evals.prompt: I just launched a new SaaS product. How do I find keywords I can actually rank for using Semrush?
evals.expected_output: Walks through using Keyword Magic Tool with seed keywords, filtering by keyword difficulty (KD%) for realistic targets, using intent filters for commercial/transactional keywords, and organizing winners into topic clusters. Mentions that KD% is relative to your domain authority and new sites should target lower difficulty.

assertions.name: keyword_tool
assertions.description: Recommends using Semrush's Keyword Magic Tool or Keyword Overview for discovering keyword opportunities

assertions.name: difficulty_filtering
assertions.description: Explains filtering by keyword difficulty (KD%) and notes that new or low-authority sites should target lower difficulty keywords

assertions.name: actionable_workflow
assertions.description: Provides a concrete workflow with multiple steps, not just a single tool recommendation — e.g., seed keyword entry, filtering, gap analysis, or organizing results

evals.prompt: My Semrush site audit shows 500+ errors. How do I prioritize what to fix?
evals.expected_output: Explains the issue hierarchy (Errors > Warnings > Notices), recommends prioritizing by category (crawlability, HTTPS, performance), identifies highest-impact fixes, and advises re-crawling to track progress. Notes that not all issues are equally important.

assertions.name: priority_framework
assertions.description: Provides a prioritization framework for site audit issues, such as fixing errors before warnings, or categorizing by impact type

assertions.name: specific_categories
assertions.description: Mentions specific types of audit issues to focus on, such as broken links, crawl errors, HTTPS issues, Core Web Vitals, or internal linking

assertions.name: tracking_progress
assertions.description: Recommends re-crawling or monitoring the health score over time to track improvement after fixes

evals.prompt: I want to use the Semrush API to build automated SEO reports. What do I need to know?
evals.expected_output: Explains API access requires Business plan ($499.95/mo), authentication via API key, unit-based pricing model, key endpoints for reporting (Domain Overview, Organic Positions), and warns about unit costs. Mentions Zapier Site Audit connector as a no-API alternative.

assertions.name: plan_requirement
assertions.description: States that Semrush API access requires the Business plan or mentions the cost barrier for API access

assertions.name: unit_pricing
assertions.description: Explains the unit-based consumption model where different endpoints have different costs, and advises budgeting or testing with small queries

assertions.name: alternative_path
assertions.description: Mentions the Zapier Site Audit connector or MCP server as alternatives that don't require the Business plan or full API access
